findings.title: `fixBadAccruals` records COMP debt that future claims never honor

best_agent_match.title: COMP debt recorded by `fixBadAccruals` is never enforced on future claims

rejected_candidates.title: Borrow and redeem paths expose stale-liquidity reentrancy across markets
rejected_candidates.reason: Only the abstract base contracts are in scope here; no concrete `doTransferOut` implementation is present that shows an attacker-controlled callback path. Without a concrete market implementation that can reenter, the claim is too speculative to report.

rejected_candidates.title: Close Factor Not Validated on _setCloseFactor
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-only parameter-setting path. Any harm depends on privileged misconfiguration by the trusted admin, which is not a standalone protocol vulnerability in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Oracle Price Manipulation Risk
rejected_candidates.reason: The report describes the trusted admin's ability to replace the oracle. That is a governance/control assumption, not an exploitable bug by unprivileged actors.

rejected_candidates.title: No Timelock on Critical Admin Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Absence of a timelock is a governance design choice rather than a code vulnerability. The candidate does not identify an unprivileged exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation Incentive Not Validated
rejected_candidates.reason: This is another admin-only misconfiguration scenario. Since the same admin can already make broader privileged changes, the missing bound is not a distinct reportable vulnerability here.

rejected_candidates.title: Interest Rate Model Can Be Changed Instantly
rejected_candidates.reason: Changing the interest rate model is an intended admin power guarded by `msg.sender == admin` plus interface checks. This is governance trust, not a protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Comptroller Can Be Changed on CTokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `_setComptroller` is an explicit admin hook and verifies the new target implements the comptroller marker. The candidate is a generic privileged-upgrade concern, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Borrow Cap Guardian Can Disable Borrowing
rejected_candidates.reason: The claim is factually incorrect for this code: `_setMarketBorrowCaps` documents and implements `0` as unlimited borrowing, not disabled borrowing. More generally, borrow-cap setting is an intended privileged control.

rejected_candidates.title: COMP Accrual Fix Function Allows Arbitrary Adjustment
rejected_candidates.reason: The function is a one-off admin-only remediation hook and the candidate relies on trusted-admin abuse. The reportable issue in this area is instead that recorded `compReceivable` debt is never enforced.

rejected_candidates.title: Unitroller Delegatecall Reentrancy Risk
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the standard upgradeable proxy delegation pattern. The candidate does not show a distinct exploit beyond compromising the trusted implementation/admin path.

rejected_candidates.title: Market Deprecation Can Be Abused for Immediate Liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: `isDeprecated` is an explicit admin-controlled deprecation mechanism. The ability to deprecate a market is an intended governance action, not an accidental bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: Pause Guardian Can Pause Critical Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: The pause guardian is an intentional emergency-control role documented in storage comments. This is a trust/governance feature, not a bug.
